findings.question: Q1
findings.summary: Devin documentation is silent on hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext; Claude SessionStart hook uses plain text output, not JSON; empirical verification blocked by self-invocation constraint

evidence.description: Devin lifecycle hooks docs document SessionStart input (source field) but no additionalContext output field
Claude Code docs explicitly document hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext for SessionStart
Existing Claude SessionStart hook uses plain text markdown output via formatHookOutput, not JSON
Code-graph SessionStart hook is registered in Claude config
Advisor packet reached identical findings for UserPromptSubmit

findings.recommendation: Rely on read_config_from.claude=true inheritance; add explicit Devin variant only if empirical testing reveals inheritance doesn't work

findings.question: Q2
findings.summary: Hook source migration to system-code-graph/hooks/ has high breaking change risk (110+ file references, .claude/settings.local.json paths, build config changes); recommend Option B (keep current location) and add Devin variant locally

evidence.description: system-code-graph has no hooks directory; advisor pattern shows skill-owned hooks
Boundary layer provides cross-skill interface; hooks depend on this layer
Build process bundles system-code-graph as sibling; migration would require build changes
110+ files reference system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks path
Critical hook registration path would need atomic update

findings.recommendation: Option B: Keep hooks in system-spec-kit/mcp_server/hooks/, add Devin variant locally; document asymmetry in ADR-001

findings.question: Q3
findings.summary: Hybrid approach recommended: try Option C (inheritance via read_config_from.claude=true) first, fallback to Option A (system-spec-kit/hooks/devin/) if inheritance fails; matches advisor packet pattern

evidence.description: Advisor packet recommended identical hybrid approach for UserPromptSubmit
Q1 uncertainty makes Option C a calculated risk

findings.recommendation: Hybrid: Phase C attempt Option C first, implement Option A if context injection fails; document both in ADR-002

findings.question: Q4
findings.summary: Startup payload contract verified in readiness-marker.ts: kind=startup, provenance fields, sectionKeys=[structural-context]; getStartupBriefFromMarker() provides clean access; import surface safe for hook location

evidence.description: buildStartupBrief() emits exact required contract
getStartupBriefFromMarker() provides clean interface

findings.recommendation: Direct call pattern: Devin variant should call getStartupBriefFromMarker() using same import path as existing variants

findings.question: Q5
findings.summary: Freshness handling: mirror Claude pattern (warn + recommend code_graph_scan, no inline refresh); Devin stop_hook_active semantics out of scope for SessionStart hook

evidence.description: Claude variant shows stale warning pattern

findings.recommendation: Mirror Claude stale-handling exactly; stop-hook relevance out of scope

findings.question: Q6
findings.summary: Plugin rename scope: PLUGIN_ID, bridge, docs, tests; no legacy env vars found; matches advisor pattern but simpler (no env alias needed)

evidence.description: No SPECKIT_*_CODE_GRAPH_* env vars found

findings.recommendation: Direct rename: spec-kit-compact-code-graph → mk-code-graph; no env var aliases needed

findings.question: Q7
findings.summary: Bridge rename: spec-kit-compact-code-graph-bridge.mjs → mk-code-graph-bridge.mjs; no duplicate found at system-spec-kit (advisor had duplicate, code-graph does not)

evidence.description: No duplicate bridge file exists

findings.recommendation: Simple rename with import path updates; no deletion needed

findings.question: Q8
findings.summary: Naming asymmetry justified: mk-code-index (MCP server) is stable tool contract; mk-code-graph (plugin) matches SKILL folder for symmetry; ADR-002 draft documents rationale

evidence.description: MCP server registered as mk_code_index

findings.recommendation: Maintain asymmetry; document in ADR-002; add explanation to SKILL.md and plugins/README.md

findings.question: Q9
findings.summary: Post-extraction surface clean: only justified boundary layer references; no accidental cross-references; sk-code compliant for TypeScript/Node surface

evidence.description: Boundary layer documented as intentional interface

findings.recommendation: No remediation needed; standard verification (typecheck, lint, vitest) should pass

findings.question: Q10
findings.summary: sk-doc baseline assessed; gaps identified (SET-UP_GUIDE empty, plugin rename refs, Devin variant docs); Phase B/C synthesis complete; convergence achieved

evidence.description: SKILL.md, README, INSTALL_GUIDE, SET-UP_GUIDE (empty), feature_catalog, manual_testing_playbook

findings.recommendation: Proceed to Phase B; populate SET-UP_GUIDE; update plugin rename refs; implement Phase C with risk mitigations
